title: You ruined someone’s special moments
text: May 3rd is my guy’s birthday, and today at exactly 10:00 AM, the moment you released the Nike Mind (black and blue fade), I hit the payment button. I was on time. I was ready. I did everything right.  For the past two weeks, I’ve been constantly checking the Nike App just to make sure I don’t miss this drop. I even saved my card details and shipping address in advance so there would be zero delay from my side.  But your app completely ruined the experience.  Every time I submitted the payment, it showed pending, then checking availability, and then suddenly said the item was unavailable. I tried multiple times, and the same thing kept happening — while the app still showed my size as available.  How does that even make sense? If I clicked exactly at 10:00 AM and submitted my payment on time, how is the stock already gone while you’re still letting me proceed to checkout?  You made me feel like I was going to get it — and then took it away at the last second.  This wasn’t just a purchase. That guy literally worked so hard to get me the best birthday gift, and it honestly hurts that I couldn’t get what he wanted because of this system. You didn’t just lose a sale — you ruined a special moment for both of us.  And it’s not even cheap — it’s around $249 in stores. For that price, at least give customers a fair and transparent experience. Please don’t play with the emotions of middle-class people who are genuinely trying to afford something special.  This is really, really sad and extremely disappointing, Nike. You seriously need to fix this system and stop misleading customers during drops.

title: I am formally escalating this issue
text: I attempted to purchase the Nike Mind 001 four times, each time setting alarms and being ready before the drop. I completed the checkout process, and my payment went into “pending” every single time — yet my orders were never completed.  This is not a matter of bad timing. This feels like a flawed or unfair system where genuine customers are unable to secure a purchase despite acting immediately.  It is unacceptable to repeatedly authorize payments, only to end up with nothing while the product sells out instantly. This raises serious concerns about the fairness and reliability of your release system.  I am requesting a clear explanation of why this keeps happening and what steps Nike is taking to ensure equal opportunity for customers.

text: To Whom It May Concern,  I am writing to bring to your attention  an issue I am experiencing with the current Nike app. I am using an iPhone 8 Plus, and unfortunately, I am no longer able to access the app due to what appears to be a compatibility or software glitch.  At this time, I am unable to upgrade to a newer device, and this situation prevents me from using the Nike app entirely. I have been a loyal Nike customer for several years, and I believe there may be many other customers experiencing the same inconvenience with older, yet still functional, devices.  I respectfully ask if there is an alternative method to access Nike services or account features without requiring the purchase of a new phone, or if this issue may be addressed in a future update.  Thank you for your time and consideration. I appreciate any assistance or guidance you can provide.   Sincerely, Lloyd Hoagland Louisville, KY 40212

title: Zero chat support response
text: So as a business owner I have literally spent tens of thousands of dollars on this app. I had issues with orders where checking my inventory I was missing a lot of orders and when checking the app the order show as “shipped” not delivered. When reaching out to chat support I would always end up having to re explain to a new representative because somehow there would always be an error or “no connection” while on WiFi. But no problem trying to place an order once kicked out of chat. But what’s more alarming is they completely started ignoring my chats.. till this day it’s been about 2 years almost and I have not gotten a single response to over 30 attempts and messages sent. I had a lot of double orders as well and when returning there’s a whole mix up of return started even though I sent the shoes already. Or it will say returned at store even though it literally has tracking details. This is very upsetting and frustrating at the same time but given the amount of support and hard earned money I’ve spent with Nike it’s very disheartening to be disrespected like this. Wouldn’t expect this from a top tier elite corporation like this.

text: First,I tried to scan the bar code  on my receipt but nothing happened. I have got to give credit to the young man that helped my wife and me. His name is Simeon and I don’t have his last name. title: Backpack
text: I ordered a Jordan air school backpack in the white color and they sent me a pink one. After this, I returned the pink one and ordered another white one and as soon as I receive the white one, it is missing the computer pouch that it is advertised to have. I ordered this backpack specifically because it only has one pocket with a computer pouch in that one pocket, which is exactly what I need for school. I don’t like it when big companies fail to even supply the bags that they have shown in the pictures because people feel like they’re getting ripped off. I know many other people that have experienced this problem as well and I know they hate the feeling when it arrives and it is missing one of the key features(pencil case, laptop pocket). mine did come with a pencil case which is ok but I expect Nike to be up to their standards on providing the specified product that they show in the images
